blind man's bluff card game app for Andr.response:
If you are looking to play **Blind Man's Bluff** (also widely known as **Indian Poker** or **Forehead Poker**) on your Android device, there are several real, existing apps available on the Google Play Store that fit this exact card game style:

### 1. **Blindex** (by Blindex)
* **Overview:** This is a dedicated, single-player adaptation of Blind Man's Bluff. 
* **Features:** If you want to play the game but don't have a group of friends around, *Blindex* lets you play head-to-head against an AI dealer. It features three difficulty levels (Easy, Normal, and Hard), with each AI opponent utilizing a different bluffing style and "tell" for you to read. 
* **Key Benefit:** It is 100% free, has no ads, no in-app purchases, and functions entirely offline.

### 2. **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc.)
* **Overview:** A mobile version of the classic forehead-card game designed for social play.
* **Features:** This app is built to be played with friends and family. It captures the core mechanic of Blind Man's Bluff: you can only see your opponent's card and must predict your victory based on their card and their facial expressions. It focuses heavily on the psychological aspect of reading your opponent's face and managing your own bluffing strategy.

### 3. **Indian poker** (by Kanbe)
* **Overview:** A lightweight, straightforward simulation of the classic card-on-forehead game.
* **Features:** This is a simple, no-frills app designed to facilitate multiplayer Indian Poker sessions. It acts as a digital dealer and card distributor for multiple people playing together in the same room. 

*Note: When searching the Google Play Store, keep in mind that "Indian Poker" and "Forehead Poker" are the most common alternative names used by developers for this specific card game.*

forehead poker app Android.response:
If you are looking for a "forehead poker" app on Android, the term can refer to two very different types of games: **Indian Poker (Blind Man's Bluff)**, where you hold a playing card to your forehead and bet without seeing your own card, or **Heads Up! style party games**, where you hold the phone to your forehead and guess words based on clues. 

The best Android apps available on the Google Play Store for both styles of gameplay include:

### 1. True "Forehead Poker" (Indian Poker / Blind Man's Bluff)
In these games, you only see your opponent's card and must use psychology, bluffing, and betting to win.

*   **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc.): This is a dedicated mobile version of the classic card-on-forehead game. It allows you to play with friends and family, focusing on reading your opponent's facial expressions and managing your betting strategy based entirely on the card you see on their "forehead".
*   **Indian poker** (by Kanbe): A straightforward, lightweight simulation of the classic game. It is designed for local multiplayer play, making it a great digital substitute for a physical deck of cards when hanging out with friends.
*   **Anipang Poker** (by Playlinks): While primarily a social casino app featuring classic card games like 7 Poker and Badugi, it also includes a dedicated **Indian Poker** mode where you can play the card-on-forehead game directly against other players online.

*(Note: While many apps like **Teen Patti Gold** are marketed as "Indian Poker", they are actually traditional 3-card poker variants rather than the card-on-forehead game).*

---

### 2. "Forehead" Party & Word Guessing Games
If you are looking for the popular party game where you hold the phone to your forehead to guess words, phrases, or celebrities, these are the top choices:

*   **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros. International Enterprises): The ultimate forehead guessing game popularized by Ellen DeGeneres. You place the phone on your forehead, and your friends must act out, sing, or shout clues to help you guess the word on the screen before the timer runs out.
*   **ForeHeads: Charades Guess Game**: A highly-rated alternative to Heads Up! that features over 48 unique decks and thousands of challenges. It is completely ad-free and perfect for family game nights.
*   **Charades & Headbands Game (What am I?)**: A highly customizable party game where you tilt the phone up to mark a correct guess or down to pass. It allows you to choose from dozens of pre-made pop culture decks or easily create your own custom cards.

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
The mechanic where you can see everyone else's cards but your own is famously used in two types of card games: **Hanabi** (a cooperative game where you work together to build a fireworks show) and **Blind Man's Bluff / Indian Poker** (a competitive betting game where you hold your card to your forehead). 

The following real, existing Android apps on the Google Play Store feature this unique gameplay style:

### 1. Boardible: Games for Groups
* **What it is:** A digital board game platform.
* **How it fits:** It includes a fully playable digital version of **Hanabi**. In this cooperative game, you hold a hand of cards facing away from you. Your teammates can see your cards and must give you limited clues (such as color or number) so you can figure out what to play or discard without looking. 
* **Best for:** Playing Hanabi with friends either online or locally.

### 2. Hanabi Online (by Ordon Team)
* **What it is:** A dedicated, free implementation of the Hanabi card game.
* **How it fits:** This app is entirely focused on the "blind card" cooperative gameplay of Hanabi. It connects to Google Play Services to let you match with other players online to build the perfect fireworks display.
* **Best for:** Players looking for a pure, standalone Hanabi experience online.

### 3. Blindex
* **What it is:** A single-player, offline Blind Man's Bluff (Indian Poker) game.
* **How it fits:** In this game, your card is dealt face-up to the AI opponents, but face-down to you. You must read the AI's "tells" and reactions to guess the strength of your own card before deciding whether to bet, call, or fold. 
* **Best for:** Solo players who want to practice reading opponents and playing Indian Poker offline.

### 4. Indian poker (by Kanbe)
* **What it is:** A local multiplayer tool/simulation app.
* **How it fits:** This app acts as a digital deck of cards designed for playing the physical party game of Indian Poker with friends in the same room. You hold your phone up to your forehead so your friends can see your card, while you try to guess what you have based on their reactions and bets.
* **Best for:** Playing the physical "forehead card game" at parties without needing a physical deck of cards.

Android card game where you hold card to.response:
The popular party game where you hold a phone to your forehead is commonly referred to as a "heads up" or "charades" style game. 

The most popular and highly-rated Android apps for this game on the Google Play Store include:

### 1. **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros. International Enterprises)
This is the official, incredibly popular game popularized by Ellen DeGeneres. 
* **How it works:** You select a category (such as Celebrities, Movies, Animals, or Accents), place the phone on your forehead with the screen facing your friends, and try to guess the word based on their clues. 
* **Controls:** Tilt the phone down if you guess correctly, or tilt it up to skip the word. It also has a feature that uses your front-facing camera to record your friends' hilarious reactions and clues while you play.

### 2. **Charades & Headbands: Guess Up** (by Cosmicode)
A fantastic, highly-rated alternative that offers a polished interface and plenty of content.
* **How it works:** It features over 100 themed decks (including Pop Culture, Netflix, Superheroes, and Harry Potter). 
* **Key Features:** It includes a "Team Mode" for larger groups, supports 25+ languages, allows you to record your gameplay, and even lets you create your own custom card decks to play with friends.

### 3. **ForeHeads: Charades Guess Game** 
A great, ad-free option designed specifically for quick party play.
* **How it works:** It features 48 unique decks and over 6,500 individual word challenges. 
* **Key Features:** It focuses on a clean, seamless user experience without intrusive ads, making it perfect for quick rounds during family game nights or trips.

### 4. **Hands Up: ForeHead Charades!**
A lively party-focused version of the game that is great for breaking the ice.
* **How it works:** Like the others, you hold the phone to your forehead while your friends act out, sing, or describe the word on the screen.
* **Key Features:** It features a wide variety of categories ranging from family-friendly topics to adult-themed decks for late-night parties.

single player bluffing card game Android.response:
Here are some of the best real, offline single-player bluffing card and dice games available on the Google Play Store:

### 1. **Bluff Card Game Offline** (by Gameyantra)
This app brings the classic card game of "Cheat" (also known as "I Doubt It" or "BS") directly to your phone. 
* **How it works:** You play cards face-down and declare their rank. You can lie about what you are playing, but if an opponent calls your bluff, the loser of the challenge has to pick up the entire discard pile. 
* **Offline Features:** It is designed specifically for offline play with no Wi-Fi required. It features smart AI opponents with dynamic difficulty, 2-player and 4-player modes, and customizable play rooms.

### 2. **Cheat! – Bluffing Card Game** 
A dedicated single-player offline card game built around the classic "Cheat" rules.
* **How it works:** You face off against three CPU opponents in fast-paced, strategic mind games where the goal is to empty your hand first. 
* **Offline Features:** It is fully playable offline and adds unique twists to the classic formula, including a title system (Liar, Honest, or Hunter) and strategic items like "Lie Counters" and "Bluff Shields" to help you outsmart the AI.

### 3. **Liar's Dice Online Multiplayer** (by YOAMB B.V.)
While this app has "online" in the title, it features a robust, fully-fleshed-out offline mode. 
* **How it works:** Based on the classic bluffing game of *Perudo* (or *Dudo*), players roll dice under a cup and take turns bidding on how many dice of a certain number are on the table. You must bluff your way through bids or call out your opponents' lies.
* **Offline Features:** It includes a dedicated "Offline AI Practice" mode where you can play against intelligent computer opponents without an internet connection.

### 4. **Poker Offline: Texas Holdem**
Texas Hold'em is the ultimate bluffing card game, and this app lets you experience it entirely offline.
* **How it works:** Play classic Texas Hold'em, place bets, go all-in, and execute bluffs against computer opponents.
* **Offline Features:** You do not need an internet connection to play. The game features scaling AI difficulty (opponents get smarter as the stakes get higher), Sit-N-Go tournaments, and no turn-time limits, allowing you to think through your bluffing strategy at your own pace.

* **Genre:** Poker Roguelike
* **Description:** A massive hit that brings illegal poker hands into a hypnotic, roguelike deckbuilder. You play poker hands to score points, but the real magic comes from buying and combining unique Joker cards that alter the rules, trigger massive multipliers, and create wild, game-breaking combos. It is highly addictive, fully offline, and perfect for mobile.

### 3. **Dawncaster: Deckbuilding RPG**
* **Genre:** Dark Fantasy Card RPG
* **Description:** Designed specifically for mobile, *Dawncaster* is played in portrait mode and features a dark fantasy aesthetic. You choose a class (like Warrior, Rogue, or Arcanist) and build your deck as you navigate a story-driven adventure. It features no microtransactions or internet requirements, making it one of the cleanest premium offline RPG experiences on Android.

### 4. **Night of the Full Moon**
* **Genre:** Dark Fairy Tale Deckbuilder
* **Description:** Based on the story of Little Red Riding Hood, this beautifully illustrated game has you venture into the Black Forest to find your missing grandmother. You will face off against various monsters in turn-based card combat. It is played vertically, features multiple classes with entirely different mechanics, and is incredibly easy to pick up and play offline.

### 5. **Card Thief**
* **Genre:** Stealth Puzzle Card Game
* **Description:** If you want something different from traditional deckbuilders, *Card Thief* is a tactical puzzle game played on a 3x3 grid of cards. You play as a thief sneaking through the shadows, avoiding guards, extinguishing torches, and stealing treasure. It requires careful planning and spatial strategy, making it a perfect offline brain-teaser.

### 6. **Pirates Outlaws**
* **Genre:** Pirate Roguelike Deckbuilder
* **Description:** Similar in gameplay to *Slay the Spire*, *Pirates Outlaws* has you navigate dangerous seas, manage your ammo (a unique resource mechanic), and battle outlaw crews. It features a charming paper-craft art style, over 15 playable characters, and multiple game modes that can all be enjoyed offline.

blind man's bluff card game app for Andr.response:
The Android app for **Blind Man's Bluff** (also known as **Indian Poker** or **Forehead Poker**) is called **Blindex**.

**Blindex** is a free, offline single-player card game where you play head-to-head against an AI dealer [1]. In this game, the AI opponent holds its card face-out against its forehead, allowing you to see it while it cannot see its own card [1]. You must read the AI's "tells" (such as flinches, smirks, or stares) to guess the value of your own hidden card [1].

Key features of **Blindex**:
*   **Offline Play:** No internet connection is required to play [1].
*   **Difficulty Levels:** Choose from three difficulty levels, each with a different AI bluffing style and tell to read [1].
*   **Cost:** It is 100% free with no ads or in-app purchases [1].
*   **Customization:** Earn points to buy cosmetic hairstyles, outfits, card decks, and tables in the shop [1].
*   **Availability:** Available worldwide on Google Play for Android [1].

You can download it directly from the Google Play Store by searching for "Blindex" [1].

*Note: Other apps like "Bluff Online" [2] or "Bluff: Cards Game" [3] refer to a different card game where players aim to get rid of their cards by lying about the cards they play, which is not Blind Man's Bluff.* [2][3]

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
The Android app you’re looking for is likely **Hanabi** (also known as **Fireworks**), a cooperative card game where players cannot see their own cards but can see everyone else’s.

- In **Hanabi**, each player holds their cards facing outward so others can see them, but they cannot see their own hand. Players must give clues to help teammates deduce what cards they hold to successfully complete the game [2].
- While the search results don’t explicitly list a Hanabi app, it is a well-known game with multiple Android versions available on Google Play Store (e.g., “Hanabi – The Card Game” by various developers).

Other cooperative card games with similar mechanics include:
- **Skyjo** (mentioned as “Skyjo” in [2]), though it’s more about hidden cards in a layout rather than not seeing your own hand.
- **Port Royal** and **Speed** are also mentioned in [2], but they don’t match the “can’t see your own card” mechanic as precisely as Hanabi.

For the most accurate experience, search for **“Hanabi card game Android”** on Google Play Store. Actual app names include:
- **Hanabi – The Card Game**
- **Fireworks: Hanabi Card Game**

These are real, existing apps available on Google Play Store that match your description.

Android card game where you hold card to.response:
The Android game you’re describing is **Heads Up!** (also known as **Charades & Headbands: Guess Up** or similar variants), where one player holds the phone to their forehead with the screen facing outward, and friends give clues to help them guess the word or phrase displayed.

- In **Heads Up!** by Warner Bros. (Ellen DeGeneres), players pick a card deck (e.g., celebrities, animals, accents), hold the phone to their forehead, and guess words based on friends’ clues by tilting the phone down to confirm or up to skip [3][6][7].
- A similar app called **Charades & Headbands Game** lets you place the device on your forehead and guess words while friends dance, sing, or mime clues [1].
- The core mechanic is the same across these apps: **hold the phone to your forehead**, **guess the word** from friends’ clues, and **tilt your head** to progress [1][3][4].

For Android, the official and most popular version is **Heads Up!** ([com.wb.headsup](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.wb.headsup)) [3].

single player bluffing card game Android.response:
For a **single-player bluffing card game** on **Android** that works **offline**, the app **"Bluff: Cards Game"** (by Oneiric Gaming) is the closest match to the bluffing mechanics you described, but **it primarily focuses on multiplayer online play** and may not offer a true single-player offline bot mode by default [1][4].

However, if you want a **verified offline single-player card game** with bluffing or guessing elements on Android, consider these excellent alternatives from Google Play:

| App Name | Why It Fits | Offline? | Single-Player? |
|----------|-------------|----------|----------------|
| **Night of the Full Moon** | Vertical roguelite with deck-building, bluffing-like guessing, and resource management | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | [3] |
| **Void Tyrant** | Deck-building roguelite with character progression and strategic bluffing elements | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | [3] |
| **Card Quest** | Puzzle-card RPG with strategic guessing and deck-building | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | [3] |
| **Spades – Offline Card Games** | Classic trick-taking game with high-level AI (no bluffing, but strong offline single-player) | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | [5] |

**Recommendation:**  
Try **"Night of the Full Moon"** first — it’s **free to try**, **completely offline**, **single-player**, and includes **bluffing/guessing mechanics** in its deck-building gameplay [3]. If you want a pure bluffing card game like the traditional "Bluff" (where you announce a rank and play face-down cards), you may need to check if **"Bluff: Cards Game"** added a bot mode in recent updates, as its current description emphasizes multiplayer [1][4].

All recommended apps are available on **Google Play Store** and work without Wi-Fi.
